tool_args.label: MTA Board Meeting September 2024 - Board Action Items

payload.title: MTA Board Action Items
payload.description: MTA Board meeting packet for September 25, 2024, including the 2025-2029 Capital Program, procurement actions, real estate items, and meeting minutes from July 31, 2024.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: MTA 2025-2029 Capital Plan
tool_args.raw_text: The proposed 2025-2029 Capital Plan includes critical investments to rebuild, improve, and expand the MTA system. The plan will enable the MTA to continue to provide frequent and reliable service by putting the system on a path to state of good repair, including investments in railcars, power, and signals.

payload.description: The proposed 2025-2029 Capital Plan includes critical investments to rebuild, improve, and expand the MTA system. It is recommended that the MTA Board approve the proposed MTA 2025-2029 Capital Plan of $68.400 billion and authorize the MTA to submit the $65.400 billion CPRB portion to the CPRB for its review and approval.

tool_args.label: Interborough Express (IBX)
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Major Projects and Expansion: $5.250 billion. The proposed 2025-2029 Capital Plan includes funding for the Interborough Express (IBX) and critical state-of-good repair investments at the Grand Central Trainshed and Terminal as part of the MTA's Grand Central Artery Initiative.

payload.description: The proposed 2025-2029 Capital Plan includes funding for the Interborough Express (IBX).

warnings: project.program is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Program names caused identity overmerge; they are relation context, not strong project identity. Suggested relation: part_of_program.

tool_args.label: Grand Central Artery Initiative
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Major Projects and Expansion: $5.250 billion. The proposed 2025-2029 Capital Plan includes funding for the Interborough Express (IBX) and critical state-of-good repair investments at the Grand Central Trainshed and Terminal as part of the MTA's Grand Central Artery Initiative.

payload.description: Critical state-of-good repair investments at the Grand Central Trainshed and Terminal.

tool_args.label: Harlem and Hudson Power Improvements Project - Pleasantville Substation
tool_args.raw_text: In support of the Harlem and Hudson Power Improvements Project - Pleasantville Substation (the "Project"), Metro-North seeks to construct a new electrical substation in the Village of Pleasantville in order to strengthen the traction power system on the Harlem Line.

payload.project_name: Harlem and Hudson Power Improvements Project - Pleasantville Substation
payload.description: Metro-North seeks to construct a new electrical substation in the Village of Pleasantville to strengthen the traction power system on the Harlem Line.

tool_args.label: East River Tunnel 1 and 2 Rehabilitation
tool_args.raw_text: Grant of permanent and temporary easements to National Railroad Passenger Corporation for the East River Tunnel 1 and 2 Rehab project at the Sunnyside Yard in Queens, NY

payload.description: Amtrak rehabilitation work to East River Tunnels 1 and 2 at Sunnyside Yard in Queens, requiring permanent and temporary easements from LIRR.

tool_args.label: Tarrytown Transit-Oriented Development - Hudson Harbor Station
tool_args.raw_text: Conveyance of property interests to Hudson Harbor LLC in support of an 88-unit multifamily TOD in Tarrytown, NY

payload.description: Ground lease of ±25,000 sq ft of MTA Property at 2 Depot Plaza, Tarrytown for an 88-unit multifamily housing project with community artist workshops and galleries, with improved access to MTA Property and Tarrytown Station.

tool_args.label: MTA Board Meeting September 25, 2024
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Board Meeting 2 Broadway 20th Floor Board Room New York, NY 10004 Wednesday, 9/25/2024 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM ET

payload.description: MTA Board meeting to approve the 2025-2029 Capital Plan and other action items.

tool_args.label: MTA Board Regular Meeting July 31, 2024
tool_args.raw_text: Joint Minutes of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority...Regular Board Meeting Minutes Wednesday, July 31, 2024 9:00 a.m. 2 Broadway New York, NY 10004

payload.description: MTA and MTA Agencies Regular Joint Committee and Board Meeting.

tool_args.label: Capital Program Committee Meeting September 23, 2024
tool_args.raw_text: Board Action Order To Date Approval Info Other 1 Capital Program Committee 9/23/24 X 2 Board 9/25/24 X

payload.description: Capital Program Committee meeting preceding Board approval of procurement actions.

tool_args.label: 2025-2029 Capital Plan total value
tool_args.raw_text: It is recommended that the MTA Board approve the proposed MTA 2025-2029 Capital Plan of $68.400 billion and authorize the MTA to submit the $65.400 billion CPRB portion to the CPRB

tool_args.label: 2025-2029 Capital Plan CPRB portion
tool_args.raw_text: authorize the MTA to submit the $65.400 billion CPRB portion of it to the CPRB for its review and approval

tool_args.label: B&T non-CPRB portion
tool_args.raw_text: the $3.000 billion non-CPRB portion proposed by MTA Bridge and Tunnels (B&T)

tool_args.label: NYCT and SIR 2025-2029 Capital Plan allocation
tool_args.raw_text: New York City Transit (NYCT) and Staten Island Railway (SIR): $47.386 billion.

tool_args.label: LIRR 2025-2029 Capital Plan allocation
tool_args.raw_text: Long Island Rail Road (LIRR): $6.005 billion.

tool_args.label: MNR 2025-2029 Capital Plan allocation
tool_args.raw_text: Metro North Railroad (MNR): $6.005 billion.

tool_args.label: MTAB 2025-2029 Capital Plan allocation
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Bus Company (MTAB): $454 million.

tool_args.label: MTA Interagency 2025-2029 Capital allocation
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Interagency: $300 million.

tool_args.label: MTA Major Projects and Expansion 2025-2029 allocation
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Major Projects and Expansion: $5.250 billion.

tool_args.label: B&T 2025-2029 Capital Plan
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Bridge & Tunnels (B&T): $3.00 billion.

tool_args.label: Federal grants estimate for 2025-2029 Capital Plan
tool_args.raw_text: federal grants, which are estimated at approximately $14 billion based on funding levels for the 2020-2024 Plan

tool_args.label: Ridership increase since Chair Lieber became Chair
tool_args.raw_text: ridership, which has increased by 50% since he became Chair three years ago

tool_args.label: Subway crime as percentage of NYC crime
tool_args.raw_text: subway crime continues to receive extensive media coverage, it constitutes only 2% of overall NYC crime

payload.scope: subway crime as share of overall NYC crime

tool_args.label: Subway crime decrease year-over-year
tool_args.raw_text: crime rates in the subway system have decreased by 7% from last year

tool_args.label: Subway on-time performance
tool_args.raw_text: subway on-time performance at 84%

tool_args.label: LIRR on-time performance
tool_args.raw_text: Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Railroad achieving 96% and 98% on-time performance, respectively

tool_args.label: MNR on-time performance
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North Railroad achieving 96% and 98% on-time performance

tool_args.label: Subway on-time performance during heat events
tool_args.raw_text: Even during the weeks in July where there were extreme heat events, subway on-time performance remained at 82%.

tool_args.label: Access-A-Ride ridership increase
tool_args.raw_text: the Access-A-Ride system, which has seen a 20% increase in ridership

tool_args.label: Access-A-Ride customer satisfaction
tool_args.raw_text: customer satisfaction has reached 78%, the highest it has ever been

tool_args.label: Subway crime decrease vs pre-COVID
tool_args.raw_text: crime rates in the subway system have decreased by ... 12% compared to pre-COVID levels

tool_args.label: Lowest felony crimes per day since 2010
tool_args.raw_text: 2024 has seen the lowest number of felony crimes per day in the subway system since 2010

payload.raw_value_text: lowest number of felony crimes per day in the subway system since 2010

tool_args.label: Budget reduction in real terms vs pre-pandemic
tool_args.raw_text: the agency's budget is 3% lower in real terms than it was before the pandemic, which has been achieved by cutting $400 million from the operating budget

tool_args.label: Operating budget cuts
tool_args.raw_text: cutting $400 million from the operating budget

tool_args.label: Commuter railroad fare evasion drop
tool_args.raw_text: evasion rates have dropped by 30% thanks to improved enforcement and data-driven strategies

tool_args.label: Bus fare evasion rate
tool_args.raw_text: bus fare evasion, which is nearing 40%

tool_args.label: Bond funding for 2025-2029 Capital Plan
tool_args.raw_text: $13 billion of MTA and Triborough Bridge & Tunnel Authority (TBTA) Bonds, which are proposed to support 19% of the proposed 2025-2029 Capital Plan

tool_args.label: Additional capital appropriations for 2025-2029
tool_args.raw_text: An additional $8 billion of capital appropriations would be available assuming the same percentage of core investments as in the 2020-2024 Plan.
The plan will enable the MTA to continue to provide frequent and reliable service by putting the system on a path to state of good repair, including investments in railcars, power, and signals.

payload.claim_text: The proposed 2025-2029 Capital Plan includes critical investments to rebuild, improve, and expand the MTA system, putting the system on a path to state of good repair, including investments in railcars, power, and signals.
payload.subject: MTA 2025-2029 Capital Plan

tool_args.raw_text: NYCT and SIR will build on successful initiatives of the 2020–2024 Capital Plan by continuing to expand ADA station accessibility to at least 60 stations and by installing modern Communications-Based Train Control (CBTC) signaling on at least 75 miles of the subway system.

payload.claim_text: NYCT and SIR will continue to expand ADA station accessibility to at least 60 stations and install modern CBTC signaling on at least 75 miles of the subway system.
payload.subject: NYCT and SIR 2025-2029 Capital Program

tool_args.raw_text: Cyclical bus replacement will continue, including the addition of 500 electric buses to our zero-emission fleet (ZEF), alongside 1,761 new clean diesel and Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) ones.

payload.claim_text: Cyclical bus replacement will continue, including the addition of 500 electric buses to the zero-emission fleet, alongside 1,761 new clean diesel and CNG buses.
payload.subject: NYCT bus fleet replacement

tool_args.raw_text: While enforcement is key, the Chair underscored the importance of ensuring that fare evasion is not a crime of poverty; and he highlighted the success of the Fair Fares program, which provides discounted fares for low-income New Yorkers.

payload.claim_text: Chair underscored the importance of ensuring that fare evasion is not a crime of poverty, highlighting the success of the Fair Fares program.
payload.subject: MTA Chair Lieber
